name: aberdeen-1996-ibm-as400-vs-microsoft-nt-server
title: IBM's AS/400 Versus Microsoft's NT Server: The Challenge That Is Not
description: Aberdeen Group analyzes Microsoft's challenge to IBM's AS/400 installed base, arguing that the AS/400 with over 350,000 installations will retain its dominance in midrange commercial computing against Microsoft's NT Server (BackOffice). Aberdeen presents four economic and technical reasons why the challenge will fail: higher total cost for NT due to fat-client PCs, lack of match with AS/400 customer culture, absence of proven migration economics, and AS/400 Advanced Series' superior 64-bit RISC capabilities — predicting the debate will be viewed as 'the challenge that was not' by 1997.
